member.name: M:Prime.BusinessLogic.FuseLogicCollection.FuseBasic.FuseStringUtilities.CreateFuseHeader(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create fuse header by processing each bit in number fuseBits. Example: If fuse bit size is 12, '1' is process first, then '2'. End result become 110000000000 which act as first item in list. Second item in list is 1 digits printing from 0-9. Eg: 210987654321.

member.name: M:Prime.BusinessLogic.FuseLogicCollection.FuseBasic.FuseStringUtilities.BuildSignificantNumber(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Build significant number. For example: if fuseBitIndexes is 94, first time going through this function shall return 49. Subsequent shall return 9.
member.param: Iterator of fuseBitIndexes in decrement order.

member.name: M:Prime.BusinessLogic.FuseLogicCollection.FuseBasic.FuseStringUtilities.ConstructHeader(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Construct header using indexes.
member.param: Determine how many digits we need to print the current process bit.
Used only on first loop, determine loop value.
If fuse bit is 12, current process bits means '1' and '2'.

member.summary: Gets or sets ratio. The ratio (multiplier) is used to expand the fuse string after fuse burn execute mask. The expansion is applied by per bit, example, string 'abc' with ratio 2 will get 'aabbcc'.

member.name: P:Prime.BusinessLogic.FuseLogicCollection.FuseBurn.Configurations.BurnPatternConfig.GapSize
member.summary: Gets or sets gap size (the number of skip 's' per data block size). Example, string 'abcd' with data block size 2 and gap size 3 will get 'abssscd'. This does not affect by Ratio.

member.summary: Decode ctv value using 3x algorithm. 1 + 1 + 1 = 1 0 + 0 + 0 = 0 Else programmed to u. Algorithm as stated above.

member.param: String of fuse string. Expect LSB->MSB. The String should consist only '0' and '1'.
String of mask. Expect MSB->LSB. The String should consist only '0', '1' and 's', 'm' should already replace by dynamic value.

member.name: M:Prime.BusinessLogic.FuseLogicCollection.FuseRead.IFuseReadHandle.GetFuseStringForItuffByFuseGroup(System.String)
member.summary: PatternMode: Apply Reverse if true. Apply FuseGroupsToHide. SimulationMode: No Reverse. Apply FuseGroupsToHide. If Reverse is True this fuse string will be Reverse. If FuseGroupsToHide is defined, this fuse string (or reversed fuse string) of the FuseGroupsToHide will be replace to '0'. Consider string of 11111010, fuse group to hide addess of 1:0. Reverse is False, fuse string will be 11111000. Reverse is True, fuse string will be 01011100.
member.returns: Fuse string with reverse (if enabled) and hide (if enabled) of the fuse group.
